volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Ghost (Section 3.2.797 - Section 3.2.847b)

sections.label: Section 3.2.797
sections.original: Quid tandem ?
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.797b
sections.original: Orat ut suadeam Philolacheti ,
sections.translation: He begs me to persuade Philolaches to let him off .

sections.label: Section 3.2.798b
sections.original: Haud opinor .
sections.translation: I don ’ t think he will . Each man reaps on his own farm [*] . If it had been bought dear ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.800
sections.original: forent , nobis istas redhibere haud liceret .
sections.translation: we shouldn ’ t have had permission to return it on his hands . Whatever profit there is , it ’ s proper to bring it home . It don ’ t , now-a-days , befit men to be showing compassion .

sections.label: Section 3.2.803
sections.original: Morare hercle ⋯ facis . subsequere .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , you are losing time while you are talking . Follow me .

sections.label: Section 3.2.803b
sections.original: Fiat .
sections.translation: Be it so .

sections.label: Section 3.2.804
sections.original: dó tibi ego operám .
sections.translation: I ’ ll give you my services .

sections.label: Section 3.2.804b
sections.original: Senex illíc est . em , tibi ádduxi hominem .
sections.translation: ( Pointing .) There ’ s the old gentleman . ( To SIMO .) Well now , I ’ ve brought you this person .

sections.label: Section 3.2.805
sections.original: Sálvom te advenisse peregre gaudeo , Theopropides .
sections.translation: I ’ m glad that you ’ ve arrived safely from abroad , Theuropides .

sections.label: Section 3.2.806
sections.original: Dei te ament .
sections.translation: May the Gods bless you .

sections.label: Section 3.2.806b
sections.original: Inspicere te aedis has velle aiebat mihi .
sections.translation: Your servant was telling me that you were desirous to look over this house .

sections.label: Section 3.2.807
sections.original: Nisi tibi est incommodum .
sections.translation: Unless it ’ s inconvenient to you .

sections.label: Section 3.2.807b
sections.original: Immo commodum . i intro atque inspice .
sections.translation: Oh no ; quite convenient . Do step in-doors and look over it .

sections.label: Section 3.2.808
sections.original: At enim mulieres —
sections.translation: But yet — the ladies —

sections.label: Section 3.2.808b
sections.original: Cave tu ullam flocci faxis mulierem .
sections.translation: Take you care not to trouble yourself a straw about any lady . Walk in every direction , wherever you like , all over the house just as though it were your own .

sections.label: Section 3.2.810
sections.original: Tamquam ?
sections.translation: Just as though —?

sections.label: Section 3.2.810b
sections.original: Ah , cave tu ílli obiectes nunc in aegritudine ,
sections.translation: Oh , take care that you don ’ t throw it in his teeth now in his concern , that you have bought it . Don ’ t you see him , how sad a countenance the old gentleman has ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.812
sections.original: Video .
sections.translation: I see .

sections.label: Section 3.2.812b
sections.original: Ergo inridere ne videare et gestire admodum ;
sections.translation: Then don ’ t seem to exult , and to be overmuch delighted ; in fact , don ’ t make mention that you ’ ve bought it .

sections.label: Section 3.2.813b
sections.original: Intellego ,
sections.translation: I understand ; and I think you ’ ve given good advice , and that it shows a humane disposition .

sections.label: Section 3.2.815
sections.original: quid nunc ?
sections.translation: ( Turning to SIMO .) What now ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.815b
sections.original: Quin tu is intro , atque otiose perspecta ut lubet .
sections.translation: Won ’ t you go in ? Look over it at your leisure , just as you like .

sections.label: Section 3.2.816
sections.original: Bene benigneque arbitror te facere .
sections.translation: I consider that you are acting civilly and kindly .

sections.label: Section 3.2.816_b
sections.original: Factum edepol volo .
sections.translation: Troth , I wish to do so . Should you like some one to show you over .

sections.label: Section 3.2.816ab
sections.original: Apage istum perductorem , non placet .
sections.translation: Away with any one to show [*] me over . I don ’ t want him .

sections.label: Section 3.2.816b
sections.original: quidquid est , errabo potius quam perductet quispiam .
sections.translation: Why ? What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.817
sections.original: Viden vestibulum ante aedis hoc et ambulacrum cuius modi ?
sections.translation: Don ’ t you see , this vestibule before the house , and the piazza , of what a compass it is ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.818
sections.original: Luculentum edepol profecto .
sections.translation: Troth , really handsome !

sections.label: Section 3.2.818b
sections.original: Age specta postes cuius modi ,
sections.translation: Well , look now , what pillars there are , with what strength they are built , and of what a thickness .

sections.label: Section 3.2.820
sections.original: Non videor vidisse postis pulchriores .
sections.translation: I don ’ t think that I ever saw handsomer pillars .

sections.label: Section 3.2.820b
sections.original: Pol mihi
sections.translation: I ’ faith , they were some time since bought by me at such a price !

sections.label: Section 3.2.821b
sections.original: Audin fuerant dicere ?
sections.translation: Don ’ t you hear — They were once ? He seems hardly able to refrain from tears .

sections.label: Section 3.2.822b
sections.original: Quanti hosce emeras ?
sections.translation: At what price did you purchase them ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.823
sections.original: Tris minas pro istis duobus praeter vecturam dedi .
sections.translation: I gave three minae for the two , besides the carriage .

sections.label: Section 3.2.824
sections.original: Hercle qui multo improbiores sunt quam a primo credidi .
sections.translation: Why , upon my word , they are much more unsound than I thought them at first .

sections.label: Section 3.2.825
sections.original: Quapropter ?
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.825b
sections.original: Quia edepol ambo ab infimo tarmes secat .
sections.translation: Because , i ’ faith , the woodworm has split them both from the bottom .

sections.label: Section 3.2.826
sections.original: Intempestivos excisos credo , id eis vitium nocet .
sections.translation: I think they were cut at an improper season ; that fault damages them ; but even as it is , they are quite good enough , if they are covered with pitch . But it was no foreign pulse-eating artisan [*] did this work . Don ’ t you see the joints in the door ? ( Pointing .)

sections.label: Section 3.2.829b
sections.original: Video .
sections.translation: I see them .

sections.label: Section 3.2.829c
sections.original: Specta , quam arte dormiunt .
sections.translation: Look , how close together they are sleeping .

sections.label: Section 3.2.830
sections.original: Dormiunt ?
sections.translation: Sleeping ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.830b
sections.original: Illud quidem , ut conivent , volui dicere .
sections.translation: That is , how they wink , I intended to say . Are you satisfied ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.831b
sections.original: Vt quidquid magis contemplo , tanto magis placet .
sections.translation: The more I look at each particular , the more it pleases me .

sections.label: Section 3.2.832
sections.original: Viden pictum , ubi ludificat cornix una volturios duos ?
sections.translation: Don ’ t you see the painting , where one crow [*] is baffling two vultures ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.833
sections.original: Non edepol video .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I don ’ t see them .

sections.label: Section 3.2.833b
sections.original: At ego video . nam inter volturios duos
sections.translation: But I can see two vultures .

sections.label: Section 3.2.834
sections.original: cornix astat , ea volturios duo vicissim vellicat .
sections.translation: The crow stands there ; it ’ s pecking at them both in turn .

sections.label: Section 3.2.835
sections.original: quaeso huc ad me specta , cornicem ut conspicere possies .
sections.translation: This way , look , prithee , towards me [*] , that yon may be able to see the crow .

sections.label: Section 3.2.836
sections.original: iam vides ?
sections.translation: ( THEUROPIDES turns towards him .) Now do you see it ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.836b
sections.original: Profecto nullam equidem illic cornicem intuor .
sections.translation: For my part , I really see no crow there .

sections.label: Section 3.2.837
sections.original: At tu isto ad vos optuere , quoniam cornicem nequis
sections.translation: But do you look in that direction , towards yourselves , since you cannot discover the crow , if perchance you may be able to espy the vultures . ( THEUROPIDES turns towards SIMO .) Now do you see them ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.839
sections.original: Omnino , ut te absolvam , nullam pictam conspicio hic avem .
sections.translation: To make an end of it with you , I don ’ t see any bird at all painted here .

sections.label: Section 3.2.840
sections.original: Age , iam mitto , ignosco : aetate non quis optuerier .
sections.translation: Well then , I give it up . I excuse you ; it is through age you cannot see .

sections.label: Section 3.2.841
sections.original: Haec , quae possum , ea mihi profecto cuncta vehementer placent .
sections.translation: These things which I can see , really they do all please me mightily .

sections.label: Section 3.2.842
sections.original: Latius démum est operae pretium ivisse .
sections.translation: Now , at length , it ’ s worth your while to move further on .

sections.label: Section 3.2.842b
sections.original: Recte edepol mones .
sections.translation: Troth , you give good advice .

sections.label: Section 3.2.843
sections.original: Eho istum , puere , circumduce hasce aedis et conclavia .
sections.translation: Ho there , boy ! take this person round this house and the apartments . But I myself would have shown you round , if I hadn ’ t had business at the Forum .

sections.label: Section 3.2.845
sections.original: Apage istum a me perductorem , nil moror ductarier .
sections.translation: Away with any one to show me over . I don ’ t want to be shown over . Whatever it is , I ’ d rather go wrong than any one should show me over .

sections.label: Section 3.2.847
sections.original: Aedis dico .
sections.translation: The house I ’ m speaking of .

sections.label: Section 3.2.847b
sections.original: Ergo intro eo igitur sine perductore .
sections.translation: Then I ’ ll go in without any one to show me over .
